task_groups.title: Write Tests: Config Loading and XDG Support

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for LoadConfig defaults and missing file behavior

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for LoadConfig validation: port, bind, external_url
details: Write test for malformed TOML returning (nil, error)

Write tests for bind: non-empty stored as-is, empty/absent replaced with '0.0.0.0'
Write tests for external_url: stored as-is for any value, empty string when absent, no error returned

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for LoadConfig validation: log level and max_body_size
details: Write table-driven tests for log level: all 7 valid values (case-insensitive), invalid values return (nil, error)
Write tests for max_body_size: valid suffixes (KB, MB, GB, case-insensitive), empty string treated as 1MB default, invalid values (0MB, -1MB, 1TB, '1 MB', plain integer) return (nil, error)
Verify MaxBodyBytes() returns correct int64 for representative inputs

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for XDG base directory support
details: Write test for XDG_CONFIG_HOME set with file present: reads from XDG path (not ./config.toml)
Write test for XDG_CONFIG_HOME set with file absent: applies defaults, does NOT fall back to ./config.toml
Write test for XDG_DATA_HOME set: Database.Path resolves to $XDG_DATA_HOME/apikit/apikit.db, no directory created
Write test for neither env var set: uses ./config.toml path and ./data/apikit.db default

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for build-time variables and config re-export
details: Write test verifying apikit.Version=='dev', apikit.Build=='dev', apikit.TokenPrefix=='ak' when built without -ldflags
Write test verifying apikit.Config is a type alias for internal/config.Config (no conversion needed)
Write test verifying apikit.LoadConfig() produces identical results to internal/config.Load()

Write test verifying Addr() returns empty string before Start() binds and after shutdown
Implement helper waitUntilListening(srv, timeout) for use across integration tests

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for graceful shutdown behavior
details: Write test verifying SIGTERM triggers graceful shutdown with drain timeout
Write test verifying drainTimeout constant is 15*time.Second in server package
Write test verifying Shutdown(ctx) with short deadline force-closes before 15s drain
Write test verifying Shutdown() is idempotent: multiple sequential and concurrent calls all return nil
Write test verifying shutdown initiation logged at info level with drain timeout duration
Write test verifying Start() returns to caller (not os.Exit()) after shutdown

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for shutdown edge cases
details: Write test verifying cancelled caller context causes force-close before 15s drainTimeout elapses

Write test verifying second Start() call after shutdown returns non-nil error immediately with 'already shut down' message

task_groups.title: Write Tests: Middleware Chain, Panic Recovery, and Request ID

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for middleware execution order
details: Write test verifying fixed middleware order by confirming 413 response includes X-Request-ID (body limit fires after request ID assignment)
Write test verifying 413 response contains X-Request-ID header with valid UUID v4
Write test verifying structured log entry status field matches actual HTTP status code for middleware-generated errors

subtasks.title: Write unit and integration tests for Request ID middleware
details: Write test verifying missing X-Request-ID header generates fresh UUID v4 in response header
Write test verifying valid UUID v4 in incoming X-Request-ID is reused in response and log entry
Write test verifying invalid/non-UUID-v4 X-Request-ID (non-UUID string, UUID v3) is discarded and new UUID v4 generated
Implement isValidUUIDv4() helper for use across tests

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for structured JSON logging

Write test verifying /healthz and /readyz logged at debug level with all five fields including duration as float64; absent at info level
Write test verifying logrus log level is configured from Config.Logging.Level at startup (warn level suppresses info entries)

details: Write tests for GET /healthz: HTTP 200, {"status":"ok"}, Cache-Control: no-cache, Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8

subtasks.title: Implement Config struct hierarchy and TOML tags
details: Define Config, ServerConfig, DatabaseConfig, LoggingConfig structs in internal/config/config.go with correct TOML tags
Add unexported maxBodyBytes int64 field to ServerConfig
Implement MaxBodyBytes() int64 getter on *ServerConfig
Ensure type alias `type Config = config.Config` is declared in root apikit package

subtasks.title: Implement Load() with defaults, TOML parsing, and XDG resolution
details: Implement Load() in internal/config/load.go: resolve config path using XDG_CONFIG_HOME or current directory
When file absent: apply all defaults and return (*Config, nil) — no fallback between XDG and cwd
When file exists: parse with github.com/BurntSushi/toml; return (nil, error) for malformed TOML
Resolve XDG_DATA_HOME for Database.Path; store path string only — no directory creation or filesystem side effects
Re-export as apikit.LoadConfig() in root package

subtasks.title: Implement config validation: port, bind, external_url, log level
details: Validate port: must be 0-65535; return (nil, error) for out-of-range values
Validate bind: accept any non-empty string as-is; replace empty/absent with '0.0.0.0'
Store external_url as-is with no validation; empty or absent stores empty string
Validate log level: case-insensitive match against [trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal, panic]; return (nil, error) for unrecognized values

subtasks.title: Implement max_body_size parsing and validation
details: Implement parseBodySize(s string) (int64, error) in internal/config: parse '<positive-integer><KB|MB|GB>' case-insensitively
Empty string treated as absent → default 1MB (1048576 bytes); no error
Invalid values (0MB, negative, space, unsupported suffix like TB, plain integer) return error
Store parsed int64 in serverConfig.maxBodyBytes; expose via MaxBodyBytes() getter

task_groups.title: Implement Root Package: Build-Time Variables, Utilities, and Error Helper

subtasks.title: Implement build-time configurable package-level variables
details: Declare `var Version = "dev"`, `var Build = "dev"`, `var TokenPrefix = "ak"` in root apikit package (e.g. apikit.go)
Update Makefile: set VERSION via `git describe --tags 2>/dev/null || echo "dev"`, BUILD via `git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "dev"`
Add LDFLAGS to Makefile build target: `-X github.com/txsvc/apikit.Version=$(VERSION) -X github.com/txsvc/apikit.Build=$(BUILD)`
Verify variables are accessible as apikit.Version, apikit.Build, apikit.TokenPrefix from external packages

subtasks.title: Implement timestamp utility functions
details: Implement NowUTC() string in apikit package: return time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
Implement FormatUTC(t time.Time) string: return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
Implement ParseUTC(s string) (time.Time, error): parse with time.Parse(time.RFC3339, s); call .UTC() on result before returning
Ensure ParseUTC returns (time.Time{}, error) for invalid input

subtasks.title: Implement APIError helper and ETag utility functions
details: Implement APIError(c echo.Context, code int, message string) error: write JSON envelope {"error":{"code":code,"message":message}}, set Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8, return c.JSON() error
Implement SetETag(c echo.Context, updatedAt time.Time): if zero time, no-op; otherwise set ETag header to `W/"<FormatUTC(updatedAt)>"`
Implement CheckETag(c echo.Context, updatedAt time.Time) bool: if zero time return false; compare If-None-Match header against `W/"<FormatUTC(updatedAt)>"`, return true if match

subtasks.title: Implement CacheCategory type and CacheMiddleware constructor
details: Declare CacheCategory int type with constants CacheNoStore (iota), CacheNoCache, CachePublic in root apikit package
Implement CacheMiddleware(cat CacheCategory) echo.MiddlewareFunc: returns middleware that sets Cache-Control header based on category
CacheNoStore → 'no-store'; CacheNoCache → 'no-cache'; CachePublic → 'public, max-age=300'
CacheMiddleware is entirely separate from Security Headers middleware; it only sets Cache-Control

checks: Root package utility tests pass: go test ./... -count=1 -run 'TestNowUTC|TestFormatUTC|TestParseUTC|TestAPIError|TestSetETag|TestCheckETag|TestCacheMiddleware|TestVersion|TestBuild|TestTokenPrefix' -v
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1
Requirements 01-REQ-15.3, 01-REQ-16.1 through 01-REQ-18.4, 01-REQ-20.1 acceptance criteria pass

subtasks.title: Implement custom panic recovery middleware
details: Implement panicRecoveryMiddleware() echo.MiddlewareFunc in server package
On panic: recover(), log at error level using logrus.WithFields with request_id, panic (fmt.Sprintf("%v", recovered)), stack_trace (string(debug.Stack()))
Call APIError(c, 500, "internal server error") to return standard envelope
Do NOT register echo's built-in middleware.Recover(); only this custom middleware handles panics
Extract request_id from Echo context value set by Request ID middleware

subtasks.title: Implement Request ID middleware
details: Implement requestIDMiddleware() echo.MiddlewareFunc
Check incoming X-Request-ID header; validate with uuid.Parse() and check version==4
If valid UUID v4: reuse as request ID; otherwise generate new UUID v4 with uuid.New()
Set request ID in X-Request-ID response header and store in Echo context for downstream use

subtasks.title: Implement Body Size Limit and Content-Type Enforcement middleware
details: Implement bodySizeLimitMiddleware(maxBytes int64) echo.MiddlewareFunc: reject requests with body > maxBytes via APIError(c, 413, ...)
Implement contentTypeEnforcementMiddleware() echo.MiddlewareFunc: for POST/PUT/PATCH only, check Content-Type == 'application/json'; reject with APIError(c, 415, ...) otherwise
GET, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS pass through without Content-Type check

subtasks.title: Implement Security Headers middleware and Logging middleware
details: Implement securityHeadersMiddleware() echo.MiddlewareFunc: set X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy: no-referrer on every response; do NOT set Cache-Control
Implement loggingMiddleware() echo.MiddlewareFunc: after handler executes, log structured JSON entry with method, path, status (int), duration (float64 ms), request_id
For /healthz and /readyz paths: log at debug level; all other paths: log at info level
Configure logrus for JSON output and apply log level from Config.Logging.Level at startup

checks: Middleware unit and integration tests pass: go test ./... -count=1 -run 'TestMiddleware|TestPanic|TestRequestID|TestBodySize|TestContentType|TestSecurity|TestLogging' -v
Race detector finds no data races in middleware tests: go test -race ./... -count=1
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
Requirements 01-REQ-9.1 through 01-REQ-14.2 acceptance criteria pass

task_groups.title: Implement Server Core: Server Struct, Lifecycle, and Handler Registration

subtasks.title: Implement Server struct and NewServer constructor
details: Define Server struct in server package: holds *echo.Echo, *echo.Group (api group), *config.Config, HealthChecker, listener, sync.Once for shutdown, started/shut-down state flags
Implement NewServer(cfg *Config, checker HealthChecker) *Server: panic with descriptive message if cfg is nil
Register middleware chain in order: panicRecovery, requestID, bodySizeLimit, contentTypeEnforcement, securityHeaders, logging
Create api group at cfg.Server.MountPoint with CacheMiddleware(CacheNoStore) pre-applied
Never call LoadConfig() internally; perform no file I/O

subtasks.title: Implement APIGroup() method and health probe route registration
details: Implement APIGroup() *echo.Group: return pre-constructed echo group; never nil; never panic even after Shutdown()
Register GET /healthz at Echo root with CacheMiddleware(CacheNoCache): return HTTP 200 {"status":"ok"}
Register GET /readyz at Echo root with CacheMiddleware(CacheNoCache): call HealthChecker if not nil; 200 {"status":"ready"} or 503 {"status":"not ready"}
Register GET /version at Echo root with CacheMiddleware(CachePublic): return {"version": apikit.Version, "build": apikit.Build, "mount_point": cfg.Server.MountPoint}

subtasks.title: Implement Start() with listener binding and Addr()
details: Implement Start() error: bind listener at cfg.Server.Bind:cfg.Server.Port; on bind failure return non-nil error without calling os.Exit()
After listener is bound, store actual address for Addr(); block with echo.Server.Serve(listener) until shutdown
Implement Addr() string: return listener address after Start() binds; return empty string before binding and after shutdown
If Start() called on already-shut-down server, return non-nil error immediately with descriptive message; do not rebind

subtasks.title: Implement Shutdown() with signal handling and idempotency
details: Declare `const drainTimeout = 15 * time.Second` in server package

Implement Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error using sync.Once: first call logs info-level message, derives context via context.WithTimeout(ctx, drainTimeout), calls echo.Shutdown(derivedCtx); subsequent calls return nil immediately
Register OS signal handler for SIGTERM and SIGINT in Start(): on signal call Shutdown(context.Background())
In-flight requests during drain window produce normal structured log entries; Start() returns nil on clean drain

checks: Server lifecycle and handler registration tests pass: go test ./... -count=1 -run 'TestServer|TestStart|TestShutdown|TestAddr|TestAPIGroup|TestHealth|TestVersion' -v
Race detector finds no data races: go test -race ./... -count=1
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
Requirements 01-REQ-1.3 through 01-REQ-7.2 and 01-REQ-19.1 through 01-REQ-19.3 acceptance criteria pass

task_groups.title: Implement cmd/apikit Reference Binary and Makefile

subtasks.title: Implement cmd/apikit/main.go reference binary
details: Create cmd/apikit/main.go: call apikit.LoadConfig(), log.Fatal on error; call apikit.NewServer(cfg, nil); call server.Start(), log.Fatal on error
Register no additional handlers beyond built-in /healthz, /readyz, /version
When started, server responds to /healthz with HTTP 200 {"status":"ok"} confirming library wiring

Add `test` target: `go test ./...`; add `lint` target: `go vet ./...`

bin/apikit responds to GET /healthz with HTTP 200 {"status":"ok"} when started

task_groups.title: Wiring Verification: End-to-End Integration and Stub Audit

subtasks.title: Trace all execution paths and verify function call chains
details: Trace 01-PATH-1 (zero-config startup): confirm LoadConfig() → NewServer() → Start() chain with no stubs; actual Echo listener binds
Trace 01-PATH-2 (readiness probe): confirm injected HealthChecker is actually called in /readyz handler; result flows to response
Trace 01-PATH-4 (graceful shutdown): confirm signal handler calls Shutdown(context.Background()); Shutdown calls echo.Shutdown with derived timeout context
Trace 01-PATH-5 (ephemeral port): confirm Addr() reads from actual listener after Start() binds; returns empty string before/after

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation across the middleware chain
details: Trace 01-PATH-3 (oversized body): confirm body limit middleware calls APIError() and its return propagates to Echo; 413 status code reaches client
Trace 01-PATH-6 (panic recovery): confirm panic is recovered, logrus error log entry includes all three fields, APIError() HTTP 500 returned to client
Verify APIError() return value (from c.JSON()) is returned by handler; write errors propagate to Echo's error handler

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real components
details: Run TS-01-SMOKE-1: zero-config startup; GET /healthz returns 200 {"status":"ok"}
Run TS-01-SMOKE-2: readiness probe with mock HealthChecker; correct 200/503 and debug-level log
Run TS-01-SMOKE-3: oversized body returns 413 with X-Request-ID and JSON envelope
Run TS-01-SMOKE-4: SIGTERM causes graceful shutdown; in-flight request completes; Start() returns nil
Run TS-01-SMOKE-5: ephemeral port; Addr() returns real port; GET /healthz succeeds; Shutdown() works
Run TS-01-SMOKE-6: panicking handler returns 500; server continues serving; error log has all three fields
Run TS-01-SMOKE-7: ETag round-trip; 200 with ETag on first request; 304 on matching If-None-Match
Run TS-01-SMOKE-8: CachePublic route override; /providers = public max-age=300; /items = no-store

subtasks.title: Audit for stub markers and dead code
details: Search for Go stub markers: `panic("not implemented")`, bare `return nil` in non-trivial paths, `// TODO`, `// FIXME`
Verify no `panic("not implemented")` in production code paths (server.go, middleware files, config/load.go, apikit.go)
Verify all middleware functions (panicRecovery, requestID, bodySizeLimit, contentTypeEnforcement, securityHeaders, logging) have complete implementations
Verify LoadConfig(), NewServer(), Start(), Shutdown(), Addr(), APIGroup(), APIError(), SetETag(), CheckETag(), NowUTC(), FormatUTC(), ParseUTC() all have non-stub implementations

subtasks.title: Verify cross-spec entry points and complete full test suite run
